title: Steady red light action
question_text: When you approach a steady red traffic light, what must you do?
explanation: A steady red light requires a complete stop before the stop line or crosswalk. You may turn right after stopping only if no sign prohibits it.

title: Flashing yellow light meaning
question_text: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
explanation: A flashing yellow light warns you to slow down and proceed with caution. It is often used at dangerous intersections.

title: Yellow light rule
question_text: When approaching a yellow light, you must stop if you can do so ______.
sentence: When approaching a yellow light, you must stop if you can do so ______.
correct_answer: A yellow light means the signal is about to turn red. If you cannot stop safely, proceed cautiously. Do not speed up to beat the light.

title: Right on red after stop
question_text: After a complete stop at a red light, when may you turn right?
opt2: Only if there is no 'NO TURN ON RED' sign
explanation: You may turn right after a complete stop unless a sign prohibits it. You must yield to pedestrians and cross traffic.

title: Double solid yellow line
question_text: What does a double solid yellow line in the center of the road mean?
opt3: Passing allowed only from one side
explanation: Double solid yellow lines separate traffic moving in opposite directions. Vehicles in both directions are prohibited from crossing these lines to pass.

title: Move over law speed
question_text: If you cannot move over for a stopped emergency vehicle on a multi-lane road, you must slow down to 20 mph ______ the posted speed limit.
sentence: If you cannot move over for a stopped emergency vehicle on a multi-lane road, you must slow down to 20 mph ______ the posted speed limit.
correct_answer: The Move Over Law requires drivers to vacate the lane closest to a stopped emergency or service vehicle. If that is not possible, reduce speed to 20 mph below the limit (or 5 mph if limit is 20 mph or less).

title: Parking downhill with curb
question_text: When parking downhill with a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
explanation: Turn your wheels toward the curb so that if the vehicle rolls, it will roll into the curb and not into traffic.

title: Parking uphill with curb
question_text: When parking uphill with a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
explanation: Turn your wheels away from the curb (to the left). If brakes fail, the car will roll into the curb.

title: Following distance seconds
question_text: You should maintain a minimum following distance of ______ seconds under normal conditions.
sentence: You should maintain a minimum following distance of ______ seconds under normal conditions.
correct_answer: The four-second rule gives you enough time to react and brake. Increase distance in rain, fog, heavy traffic, or when towing.

title: BAC limit over 21
question_text: What is the legal Blood Alcohol Concentration (BAC) limit for drivers age 21 or older in Florida?
explanation: The legal limit is 0.08%. For drivers under 21, any detectable alcohol (0.02% or more) results in suspension under Zero Tolerance law.

title: Zero tolerance for minors
question_text: If a driver under 21 is found with a BAC of 0.02% or higher, what happens?
explanation: Under Florida's Zero Tolerance law, any driver under 21 with BAC of 0.02% or higher will have their driving privilege suspended for six months.

title: Headlights required times
question_text: Florida law requires you to use headlights from ______ to ______.
sentence: Florida law requires you to use headlights from ______ to ______.
correct_answer: Headlights must be on between sunset and sunrise and any time conditions (rain, fog, smoke) reduce visibility.

title: Passing a stopped school bus
question_text: On a two-lane road, what must you do when a school bus stops with red lights flashing?
explanation: You must stop and remain stopped until the bus withdraws its stop signal and children are clear. On a divided highway with a raised barrier, only traffic going the same direction must stop.

title: Implied consent law
question_text: Implied Consent Law
sentence: When you sign a Florida driver license, you automatically agree to submit to a breath, blood, or urine test if lawfully arrested for DUI.
definition: If you refuse to take the test, your license will be suspended for one year (first refusal). A second refusal results in an 18-month suspension and a first-degree misdemeanor.
opt1: Refusing does not avoid DUI charges; it adds additional penalties.
opt2: Always comply if an officer has reasonable cause to believe you are impaired.

title: Insurance required in Florida
question_text: What two types of insurance coverage must Florida vehicle owners maintain?
explanation: Florida's No-Fault Law requires Personal Injury Protection (PIP) and Property Damage Liability (PDL), each with a minimum of $10,000 coverage.

title: Pedestrian right-of-way
question_text: When a pedestrian is in a marked or unmarked crosswalk, motorists must ______.
sentence: When a pedestrian is in a marked or unmarked crosswalk, motorists must ______.
correct_answer: You must stop and remain stopped for pedestrians crossing at any intersection or crosswalk. Never overtake a vehicle stopped for pedestrians.

title: Speed limit in residential area
question_text: Unless otherwise posted, what is the maximum speed limit in a residential area in Florida?
explanation: The standard speed limit in municipal, business, or residential areas is 30 mph unless signs indicate otherwise.

title: Maximum speed on limited access highway
question_text: What is the maximum speed limit on Florida limited access highways (interstates, turnpikes)?
explanation: The maximum speed limit is 70 mph unless posted lower. Minimum speed on such highways is 50 mph.

title: Following distance for trucks
question_text: A truck towing another vehicle must not follow within ______ feet of another truck or vehicle in tow.
sentence: A truck towing another vehicle must not follow within ______ feet of another truck or vehicle in tow.
correct_answer: Florida law prohibits following within 300 feet (except in cities/towns or when passing). This allows safe stopping distance.

title: Approaching a stopped school bus on divided highway
question_text: You are on a divided highway with a raised barrier. A school bus on the opposite side stops with red lights. Do you have to stop?
opt3: Yes, but only if children are visible
explanation: If the highway is divided by a raised barrier or unpaved median at least five feet wide, traffic going the opposite direction does not have to stop. Painted lines are not considered barriers.

title: No-Fault Law explanation
question_text: Florida's No-Fault Law
sentence: Florida is a no-fault state. Every driver must carry Personal Injury Protection (PIP) that pays for their own injuries regardless of who caused the crash.
definition: Minimum coverage: $10,000 PIP and $10,000 Property Damage Liability (PDL).
opt1: If you are injured, your own insurance pays up to the limit before you can sue the other driver (except for serious injuries).
opt2: Always keep proof of insurance in your vehicle – a Florida Insurance ID Card or electronic proof on your phone.

title: Texting while driving points
question_text: Texting while driving adds ______ points to your driving record.
sentence: Texting while driving adds ______ points to your driving record.
correct_answer: If texting occurs in a school zone, an additional 3 points are added. If it results in a crash, 6 points are added.

title: Using high beams at oncoming traffic
question_text: Within how many feet of an oncoming vehicle must you dim your high beam headlights?
explanation: You must dim your high beams to low beams within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle and within 300 feet when following another vehicle.

title: Parking near a fire hydrant
question_text: What is the minimum distance you must park from a fire hydrant?
explanation: It is illegal to park within 15 feet of a fire hydrant.

title: Minimum bicycle passing distance
question_text: When passing a bicyclist, Florida law requires a minimum clearance of ______ feet.
sentence: When passing a bicyclist, Florida law requires a minimum clearance of ______ feet.
correct_answer: You must give at least three feet of space. On a two-lane road, pass only when safe, as you would any slow-moving vehicle.

title: Yield sign meaning
question_text: What must you do at a yield sign?
opt4: Only stop if there is cross traffic
explanation: A yield sign means you must slow down and give the right-of-way to cross traffic or pedestrians. You only stop if necessary.

title: Driving under influence penalties
question_text: DUI Penalties in Florida
sentence: First DUI conviction: fine $500-$1,000 (higher if BAC .15+ or minor in vehicle), license revocation minimum 180 days, 50 hours community service, 12-hour DUI school.
definition: Second offense within 5 years: fine $1,000-$2,000, jail up to 9 months, license revocation at least 5 years.
opt1: Third offense within 10 years: third-degree felony, fine $2,000-$5,000, license revocation minimum 10 years.
opt2: Ignition interlock devices may be required. A DUI conviction stays on your record for 75 years.

title: Learner's license night driving hours
question_text: After holding a learner's license for three months, you may drive until ______ pm.
sentence: After holding a learner's license for three months, you may drive until ______ pm.
correct_answer: During the first three months, learner's license holders may only drive during daylight hours. After three months, they may drive until 10 pm.

title: Minimum driving experience for learner's license
question_text: How many total hours of driving experience must a learner's license holder have before getting a Class E driver license?
explanation: You must have at least 50 hours of supervised driving, of which 10 hours must be at night. A parent or guardian must certify this.

title: Left lane usage on multi-lane highway
question_text: On a road with two or more lanes in your direction, when should you use the left lane?
explanation: The left lane is for passing or turning left. Slower traffic must keep right. Florida law requires moving out of the left lane when being overtaken.

title: Penalty for littering more than 15 pounds
question_text: Throwing more than 15 pounds of litter from a vehicle is a first-degree misdemeanor with a fine up to $______.
sentence: Throwing more than 15 pounds of litter from a vehicle is a first-degree misdemeanor with a fine up to $______.
correct_answer: Fine up to $1,000 plus 3 points on your driving record. The court may also require litter pickup along roadways.

title: DUI refusal suspension period
question_text: If you refuse to submit to a breath, blood, or urine test after a DUI arrest, how long is your license suspended?
explanation: First refusal results in automatic 1-year suspension. Second refusal gives 18-month suspension and a first-degree misdemeanor charge.

title: Stop sign identification
question_text: What does this red, octagonal sign require you to do?
explanation: The stop sign requires a full stop at the stop line or before the crosswalk. Proceed only when safe.

title: Points to suspend license
question_text: If you accumulate 12 points within a 12-month period, your license will be suspended for ______ days.
sentence: If you accumulate 12 points within a 12-month period, your license will be suspended for ______ days.
correct_answer: 12 points in 12 months = 30-day suspension. 18 points in 18 months = 3-month suspension. 24 points in 36 months = 1-year suspension.

title: Curfew for 16-year-old licensed driver
question_text: A 16-year-old with a Class E driver license may NOT drive between which hours unless driving to/from work or with a licensed adult 21+?
explanation: 16-year-olds may not drive from 11 pm to 6 am without an exception. 17-year-olds have a curfew from 1 am to 5 am.

title: Vehicle registration for new residents
question_text: If you move to Florida and accept employment, how many days do you have to register your vehicle?
explanation: You must obtain a Florida registration and license plate within 10 days after beginning employment or enrolling a child in a Florida public school.

title: Move over law on two-lane road
question_text: If you cannot move over for a stopped emergency vehicle on a two-lane road, you must slow down to 20 mph below the limit, or to ______ mph if the limit is 20 mph or less.
sentence: If you cannot move over for a stopped emergency vehicle on a two-lane road, you must slow down to 20 mph below the limit, or to ______ mph if the limit is 20 mph or less.
correct_answer: On a two-lane road, reduce speed to 20 mph below the posted limit. If the limit is 20 mph or less, you must travel at 5 mph.

title: Hydroplaning prevention
question_text: What is hydroplaning and how to avoid it?
sentence: Hydroplaning occurs when your tires ride on a thin layer of water, losing contact with the road. You lose steering and braking control.
definition: It most often happens at speeds above 35 mph. To prevent hydroplaning: reduce speed in rain, maintain proper tire tread depth, and avoid sudden braking or steering.
opt1: If you hydroplane: ease off the gas, do not brake, and steer straight until you regain traction.
opt2: Low tire pressure and worn tread increase risk. Check your tires regularly.

title: Following emergency vehicle distance
question_text: How close may you follow a fire truck that is responding to an emergency?
explanation: It is illegal to follow within 500 feet of a fire truck or any emergency vehicle that is responding to an emergency.

title: Parking distance from railroad crossing
question_text: It is illegal to park within ______ feet of a railroad crossing.
sentence: It is illegal to park within ______ feet of a railroad crossing.
correct_answer: You must not park within 50 feet of the nearest rail of a railroad crossing.

title: When to use low beams in fog
question_text: When driving in heavy fog, what headlight setting should you use?
explanation: Low beams reduce glare and reflect less off water droplets. High beams worsen visibility. Also use fog lights if available.

title: Minimum tread depth for tires
question_text: What is the minimum legal tread depth for tires in Florida?
explanation: Tires must have at least 2/32 inch tread depth. However, the handbook recommends at least 3/32 inch for safety. Use the penny test.

title: Pedestrian crossing signal
question_text: When the pedestrian signal shows a solid 'DON'T WALK' or a raised hand, you ______ begin crossing.
sentence: When the pedestrian signal shows a solid 'DON'T WALK' or a raised hand, you ______ begin crossing.
correct_answer: Never enter the crosswalk during the 'DON'T WALK' phase. If you are already crossing when the flashing hand appears, finish crossing quickly.

title: Driving without insurance penalty
question_text: What can happen if you drive without required PIP/PDL insurance in Florida?
explanation: Your driver license and license plate can be suspended for up to three years. Reinstatement fees range from $150 to $500.

title: Road rage handling
question_text: What to do if another driver exhibits road rage
sentence: Stay calm, do not make eye contact or gestures. Slow down and let the aggressive driver pass. If necessary, call *FHP (*347) or 911.
definition: Do not engage; avoid confrontation. If followed, drive to a police station or crowded area. Never get out of your vehicle.
opt1: Road rage includes tailgating, cutting off, flashing lights, yelling, or using weapons. It is dangerous and illegal.
opt2: Being a defensive driver helps avoid triggering road rage in others. Use turn signals, don't block passing lane, and apologize if you make a mistake.

title: School zone speed limit
question_text: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in a school zone is ______ mph.
sentence: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in a school zone is ______ mph.
correct_answer: School zone speed limits are typically 20 mph during specified hours or when lights are flashing. Fines are doubled in school zones.

title: Hand signal for left turn
question_text: What is the correct hand signal for a left turn?
explanation: Extend your left arm horizontally out the driver's window. Hand signals are required when turn signals are broken.

title: Hand signal for right turn
question_text: What is the correct hand signal for a right turn?
explanation: Extend your left arm upward with elbow bent at 90 degrees. Bicyclists may use right arm out.

title: License revocation for racing
question_text: Conviction for racing on the highway will result in ______ of your driver license.
sentence: Conviction for racing on the highway will result in ______ of your driver license.
correct_answer: Racing on the highway is a serious offense that leads to mandatory license revocation. It is listed among reasons for revocation in the handbook.

title: Sharing lane with motorcycle
question_text: May a car share the same lane with a motorcycle?
opt2: No, motorcycles are entitled to the full lane
explanation: Motorcycles have the same rights as any vehicle. You must not attempt to share a lane; the motorcyclist needs the entire lane for safety.

title: 4-second following rule
question_text: How to maintain a safe following distance
sentence: Watch when the rear of the vehicle ahead passes a stationary marker (sign or pole). Count: one-thousand-one, one-thousand-two, one-thousand-three, one-thousand-four.
definition: If you reach the marker before finishing the count, you are following too closely. Reduce speed and repeat.
opt1: Increase following distance in rain, fog, heavy traffic, when towing a trailer, or when following motorcycles or large trucks.
opt2: The 4-second rule works for most passenger cars at normal speeds. For larger vehicles or adverse conditions, increase to 5-6 seconds.

title: Leaving scene of crash with injury
question_text: Leaving the scene of a crash that results in serious bodily injury is a ______ degree felony.
sentence: Leaving the scene of a crash that results in serious bodily injury is a ______ degree felony.
correct_answer: Penalties: 2nd degree felony for serious injury, 1st degree felony (minimum 4 years prison) if death occurs. License revoked for at least 3 years.

title: Who must wear a seat belt?
question_text: Which occupants are required by Florida law to wear a seat belt?
opt3: All front seat occupants and passengers under 18
explanation: Florida law requires drivers, front seat passengers, and all passengers under age 18 to wear safety belts. Passengers 18+ in rear seats are not required by law but are strongly encouraged.

title: Child car seat requirements
question_text: What are the car seat requirements for children under age 4?
explanation: Children under age 4 must be in a federally approved child safety seat. Children ages 4-5 must be in a safety seat or booster seat.

title: Parking distance from intersection
question_text: Parking is prohibited within ______ feet of an intersection.
sentence: Parking is prohibited within ______ feet of an intersection.
correct_answer: You cannot park within 20 feet of an intersection. Also prohibited within 15 feet of a fire hydrant, 30 feet of a stop sign or traffic signal, and 50 feet of a railroad crossing.

title: What to do when emergency vehicle approaches
question_text: When you hear a siren or see flashing lights behind you
sentence: Immediately pull over to the closest edge of the roadway and stop. Do not block intersections. Remain stopped until the emergency vehicle has passed.
definition: If you are in an intersection, drive through it first, then pull over. Never follow an emergency vehicle closely (within 500 feet).
opt1: Yield to police, fire, ambulance, and other emergency vehicles using audible or visual signals.
opt2: Check your mirrors often so you are not surprised. Signal and move to the right as soon as it is safe.

title: Turn signal distance
question_text: How far before a turn must you activate your turn signal?
explanation: You must signal at least 100 feet before making a turn or lane change. This gives other drivers time to react.

title: Minimum speed on 70 mph highway
question_text: When the posted speed limit is 70 mph, the minimum speed limit is ______ mph.
sentence: When the posted speed limit is 70 mph, the minimum speed limit is ______ mph.
correct_answer: Driving too slowly can be dangerous and illegal. On highways with a 70 mph limit, the minimum is 50 mph unless conditions require slower speed.

title: Open container law
question_text: Is it legal for a driver to have an open container of alcohol in the passenger area?
explanation: Open containers of alcohol in the passenger area are illegal for drivers. They must be sealed and kept in the trunk or behind the last seat.

title: Driving with expired license
question_text: What is the consequence of driving with an expired driver license?
explanation: Driving with an expired license is illegal and treated similarly to driving without a license. You can be fined and ticketed.

title: Points for speeding 16+ mph over
question_text: Exceeding the speed limit by 16 mph or more adds ______ points to your driving record.
sentence: Exceeding the speed limit by 16 mph or more adds ______ points to your driving record.
correct_answer: Speeding 1-15 mph over limit = 3 points. 16 mph or more over = 4 points. Speeding resulting in a crash = 6 points.

title: Right-of-way at intersections
question_text: Who goes first at a 4-way stop?
sentence: The first vehicle to arrive at a 4-way stop has the right-of-way. If two or more arrive at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.
definition: At a two-way stop, the vehicle on the through road has right-of-way. Turning vehicles must yield to vehicles going straight.
opt1: Always yield to pedestrians and bicyclists in crosswalks. Even if you have right-of-way, you must do everything possible to avoid a crash.
opt2: If you are unsure, make eye contact with other drivers and wave them through. Safety is more important than strict right-of-way.

title: Driving with a suspended license
question_text: What is the penalty for driving with a suspended license?
opt2: First-degree misdemeanor, up to 1 year jail and $1,000 fine
explanation: Driving while license suspended is a criminal offense, not just a traffic infraction. Penalties can include jail time.

title: Hand signal for stop or slow
question_text: The hand signal for stop or slow is left arm ______.
sentence: The hand signal for stop or slow is left arm ______.
correct_answer: Extend your left arm downward with palm facing rear. Use when brake lights are not working.

title: When may you pass on the right?
question_text: When is passing on the right legal?
opt2: When the vehicle ahead is turning left and there is a clear lane
explanation: Passing on the right is allowed only when there are two or more lanes in your direction, or when the vehicle ahead is making a left turn and you have a paved lane to pass.

title: What to do if you miss your exit on an expressway
question_text: What should you do if you miss your exit on a limited access highway?
explanation: Never back up or turn around. Continue to the next exit, then re-enter the highway going the opposite direction.

title: Learner's license minimum age
question_text: The minimum age to obtain a learner's license in Florida is ______ years old.
sentence: The minimum age to obtain a learner's license in Florida is ______ years old.
correct_answer: You may apply for a learner's license at age 15 after completing the TLSAE course and passing the vision and knowledge exams.

title: Drowsy driving dangers
question_text: Why drowsy driving is as dangerous as drunk driving
sentence: Drowsiness slows reaction time, impairs judgment, and can cause micro-sleeps where you briefly lose consciousness. Driving after 18 hours awake is similar to driving with a BAC of 0.05%.
definition: Warning signs: yawning, drifting from lane, missing exits, difficulty keeping eyes open.
opt1: Prevention: Get 7-8 hours of sleep before long trips. Take a break every 100 miles or 2 hours. If sleepy, pull over and nap for 20 minutes. Use caffeine or switch drivers.
opt2: The two main causes: lack of quality sleep and driving during normal sleeping hours.

title: When must you stop for a school bus on a divided highway?
question_text: On a highway divided by a raised median, a school bus on the opposite side has red lights flashing. Must you stop?
opt2: No, only if children are crossing
opt3: No, traffic on the opposite side does not stop
opt4: Yes, but only if bus is on your side
explanation: If the highway is divided by a raised barrier or unpaved median at least 5 feet wide, vehicles going the opposite direction do not have to stop. Painted lines do not count.

title: TLSAE course requirement
question_text: Before applying for a learner's license in Florida, you must complete the ______ course.
sentence: Before applying for a learner's license in Florida, you must complete the ______ course.
opt4: Traffic Law and Substance Abuse Education (TLSAE)
correct_answer: The TLSAE course is required for anyone who has never held a driver license in any state or country. Driver education at a certified school may substitute.

title: Maximum fine for first speeding 50+ mph over limit
question_text: What is the maximum fine for exceeding the speed limit by more than 50 mph?
explanation: First offense: fine up to $1,000. Second offense: fine up to $2,500. Fines are doubled in school and construction zones.

title: Headlights with wipers law
question_text: Florida law requires headlights to be turned on when your windshield wipers are in use. True or false?
explanation: You must turn on your headlights whenever you turn on your windshield wipers due to rain, fog, or other low-visibility conditions.

title: Blood draw for DUI serious injury
question_text: In DUI cases involving death or serious injury, a blood test may be taken ______ your consent.
sentence: In DUI cases involving death or serious injury, a blood test may be taken ______ your consent.
correct_answer: If a crash involves death or serious bodily injury, law enforcement can require a blood test even if you are unconscious or refuse.

title: What to do at a railroad crossing
question_text: Safe behavior at railroad crossings
sentence: Slow down, look and listen for trains. Never stop on tracks. If gates are down or lights flashing, stop at least 15 feet from the nearest rail.
definition: Do not go around lowered gates – it is illegal and extremely dangerous. If your vehicle stalls on tracks, get everyone out immediately and run away at a 45-degree angle toward the train.
opt1: A train cannot stop quickly; it may take over a mile to stop. Always expect a train.
opt2: If there is more than one track, make sure all tracks are clear before crossing.

title: Minimum age for motorcycle endorsement
question_text: What is the minimum age to get a motorcycle endorsement or Motorcycle Only license?
explanation: You must be at least 16 years old to operate a motorcycle on public roads. If under 18, you must hold a learner's license for 12 months with no moving violations.

title: Insurance reinstatement fee for first PIP suspension
question_text: The first suspension for failure to maintain PIP insurance carries a reinstatement fee of $______.
sentence: The first suspension for failure to maintain PIP insurance carries a reinstatement fee of $______.
correct_answer: First suspension: $150; second within 3 years: $250; third: $500. If the suspension resulted from a DUI conviction, fees are the same but follow DUI reinstatement procedures.

title: When may a driver use a handheld device in a school zone?
question_text: Is it legal to use a handheld electronic device while driving in a school zone?
explanation: Using a handheld device in a school crossing, school zone, or work zone is prohibited and adds 3 points to your driving record (in addition to the primary offense).

title: Vision standard for unrestricted license
question_text: What is the minimum vision (with or without corrective lenses) to pass the vision exam without referral?
explanation: 20/40 or better in each eye meets standards. 20/50 or worse requires eye specialist referral. Minimum field of vision is 130 degrees.

title: Parking on a hill without curb
question_text: When parking uphill without a curb, turn your wheels to the ______.
sentence: When parking uphill without a curb, turn your wheels to the ______.
correct_answer: Without a curb, turn your wheels to the right so that if the car rolls, it goes off the road rather than into traffic.

title: Who is exempt from safety belt law?
question_text: Which of the following is exempt from Florida's safety belt requirement?
explanation: Newspaper home delivery employees while delivering newspapers are exempt. Also those with physician-certified medical conditions.

title: How to handle a tire blowout
question_text: Steps to take if you have a tire blowout
sentence: 1. Take your foot off the gas pedal – do not brake. 2. Grip the steering wheel firmly and steer straight. 3. Allow the vehicle to slow down gradually. 4. When the car is under control, brake softly. 5. Pull completely off the roadway.
definition: Braking hard or jerking the steering wheel can cause a rollover or loss of control. Do not slam on brakes.
opt1: Blowouts often feel like a sudden pull to one side. Stay calm.
opt2: Regular tire pressure checks and inspecting for wear can prevent blowouts.

title: Permitted window tint reflectivity
question_text: What is the maximum light reflection allowed for side windows?
explanation: Side windows may not reflect more than 25% of light. Rear windows may not reflect more than 35%. Mirrored appearance is prohibited.

title: License revocation for DUI first offense
question_text: A first DUI conviction results in license revocation for a minimum of ______ days.
sentence: A first DUI conviction results in license revocation for a minimum of ______ days.
correct_answer: Minimum 180-day revocation. If BAC was .15 or higher or a minor was in the vehicle, revocation may be longer and fines increase.

title: Who has right-of-way at a funeral procession?
question_text: Do other drivers have to yield to a funeral procession?
opt2: No, they have no special rights
explanation: You must yield the right-of-way to funeral processions. Vehicles in the procession may follow the lead vehicle through intersections regardless of traffic signals.

title: What does a pentagon-shaped sign mean?
question_text: What does a pentagon-shaped (house-shaped) sign indicate?
explanation: A pentagon sign indicates a school zone or school crossing. Be alert for children.

title: Penalty for passing a school bus with injury
question_text: Passing a stopped school bus that results in serious injury or death carries a fine of $______.
sentence: Passing a stopped school bus that results in serious injury or death carries a fine of $______.
correct_answer: Fine $1,500, plus 6 points, 120 hours community service in a trauma center, license suspension minimum 1 year, and victim impact panel attendance.

title: ABS vs conventional braking
question_text: Difference between Anti-lock Brake System (ABS) and conventional brakes
sentence: ABS: Press hard on brake pedal, hold steady, and steer. The system pumps brakes automatically (many times per second). Do not pump.
definition: Conventional brakes: Pump the brake pedal rapidly to avoid wheel lock-up and skidding.
opt1: If you feel pulsation in the pedal with ABS, that is normal. Continue holding steady pressure.
opt2: Check your owner's manual or look for the ABS light on the dashboard to know which system your vehicle has.

title: When may a driver use high beams?
question_text: When is it legal to use high beam headlights?
opt3: On dark roads with no oncoming traffic
explanation: High beams are allowed on dark roads when no oncoming traffic is within 500 feet and you are not following within 300 feet. Do not use in fog.

title: Parking distance from stop sign
question_text: You must not park within ______ feet of a stop sign.
sentence: You must not park within ______ feet of a stop sign.
correct_answer: Parking is prohibited within 30 feet of any flashing signal, stop sign, or traffic signal.

title: What is a "No Zone"?
question_text: What does 'No Zone' refer to in commercial vehicle safety?
opt1: Area where cell phones are banned
explanation: No Zones are large blind spots around trucks and buses – front, rear, and both sides. Stay out of these areas to be seen by the driver.

title: How to drive through a roundabout
question_text: Rules for driving in a roundabout
sentence: Approach: yield to traffic already in the roundabout. Enter when there is a safe gap. Drive counter-clockwise. Do not stop inside.
definition: Choose lane before entering: right lane for right turns or straight; left lane for left turns or U-turns. Signal before exiting.
opt1: Yield to pedestrians in crosswalks. Bicyclists may take the lane or use sidewalk.
opt2: Never change lanes in a roundabout. If you miss your exit, go around again – do not back up.
